topic: Gyroscopic precession rate — c3 patches all landed; new defender-preemption text is strong, two minor nits on the new content
change_summary: detail_evidence grew from 1900 to 2492 chars (+31.2%) since c3 (2026-05-05). Investigation: the growth is the integration of c3's advocate_mode/HOLE-003 recommendation (preempt the 'we meant acceleration on purpose' defense). The c3 duplicated-sentences major HOLE-001 has been fully fixed (no duplicates remain). The c3 minor URL HOLE (generic IAU homepage) has been upgraded to the IAU 2006 Resolution B1 PDF. Three of three c3 holes resolved; two minor new nits surface on the new prose; sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-19T01:10:25Z; obe_skipped=1 (WIN-012 already re-reviewed since audit).
our_argument_summary: Three-failure argument is intact and strengthened. (1) Units error (rad/s² is angular acceleration, not the angular velocity required for a precession 'rate') — the new prose now explicitly engages and defeats the 'we meant acceleration on purpose' rescue by computing the actual IAU 2006 P03 angular acceleration (~10⁻²⁴ rad/s², twelve orders of magnitude below 4.87e-12) and showing the dome's value would trigger a 2×10⁷ runaway over one year that VLBI/LLR would have detected; further, the dome's own page text calls it a 'rate' (matching the 25,772-yr cycle), so the rescue is a defender retreat the published claim does not actually make. (2) ~37% numerical mismatch against IAU 2006 angular velocity (7.7257e-12 rad/s) even under the charitable units-fix. (3) No dome-geometry derivation; raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 presents the value as a bare assertion with no calculation path. Verdict 'Misleading' continues to hold strongly; arguably has been moved closer to 'Refuted by Data' by the new content, but the structural post-hoc/transcription critique in detail_verdict_text keeps the milder verdict appropriate (we attack the prediction-status, not just the number).

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 (static WIN-021 entry, no code path)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline. raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 reads: 'WIN-021 — Gyroscopic Precession Rate CONFIRMED (4.87e-12 rad/s^2)' — a bare assertion. No formula, no input parameters, no derivation step appears in any dome raw-text file. There is no monitor.py in this snapshot of the dome repo's published raw-text (the dome appears to have removed or relocated automation scripts), so no live fetch comparison exists either. 'monitoring: none' tag remains accurate.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Bare assertion, neither hardcoded-and-compared nor live-fetched. No pred==obs check exists in published code.
data_sources_used: None — no external data fetched for this WIN
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Our text now says 'monitor.py does not include gyroscopic precession in its multi-domain automated audit engine.' Accurate against the current raw-text snapshot (monitor.py is not present in raw-text/). Mild forward-looking note: if/when the dome republishes a monitor.py script, this clause may need re-verification, but as of this cycle it is correct.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 is the only WIN-021 mention across all raw-text files. No domain, no fetch, no comparison. Confirmed by grep across raw-text/ — 'precess' appears only in WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis context) and WIN-051 (apsidal precession passing mention); no automated check for the WIN-021 value exists.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics value (50.2881 arcsec/yr → 7.7257e-12 rad/s, IAU 2006) does NOT match dome's 4.87e-12 rad/s². The dome is making a failed independent prediction, not relabeling a successful one. (If the value matched, this would be relabels_standard:true.)
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Earth's axial precession has been measured since Hipparchus; the IAU 2006 P03 model dates to Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront (2003) A&A 412, 567. The dome adopted a value in the right order-of-magnitude neighborhood and labeled it confirmed; the Part 3.1b trivial-transcription test applies regardless of whether the value happens to match. The detail_verdict_text correctly invokes this structural critique.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 contains no derivation. Dome elsewhere cites tau/I framing but never produces intermediate steps connecting disc_radius=20,015 km / firmament_height=9,086 km / sun_altitude=5,733 km to 4.87e-12. Even the c4 prose acknowledges this: 'The model defines the dome shape to reproduce the precession rate, then counts the fit as a prediction — circular reasoning.'

holes_found.description: QUANTITATIVE PRECISION NIT on the new content. The text says 'the IAU 2006 P_03 model as the t² coefficient of the longitudinal expression (≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s².' The t² coefficient of psi_A in Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront (2003) is −1.0790069 arcsec/century² — magnitude ≈1, correct. BUT: the angular acceleration is d²psi_A/dt² = 2 × (t² coefficient) = 2.158 arcsec/century², because the polynomial coefficient of t² is α/2 in a Taylor expansion psi = ωt + (α/2)t². So the conversion 'the t² coefficient (≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s²' silently conflates the polynomial coefficient (5.25×10⁻²⁵ rad/s² when converted directly) with the angular acceleration (1.05×10⁻²⁴ rad/s², which IS the magnitude the text quotes). Both readings round to 'roughly 10⁻²⁴' and the 'twelve orders of magnitude' claim holds either way (12.7 for the actual acceleration, 13.0 for the bare coefficient), so the argument doesn't break. But a physicist nitpicking the text could note we elided the factor-of-2. Suggested fix: replace 'the t² coefficient of the longitudinal expression (≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴ rad/s²' with 'the second derivative of the longitudinal expansion (twice the t² coefficient of ≈1 arcsec/century², i.e. ≈2 arcsec/century²), which converts to ≈10⁻²⁴ rad/s²'. Severity minor because the math result we quote is in fact correct for the acceleration the argument needs, and the rhetorical force is unchanged.
holes_found.recommendation: Reword the t² parenthetical to make the d²/dt² factor explicit. Optional but cleaner.

holes_found.description: CITATION GAP on the new IAU 2006 P03 reference. The only hyperlink in the WIN-021 detail_evidence is to https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf — which substantiates the angular VELOCITY (50.2881 arcsec/yr) but does NOT substantiate the t² coefficient or the P03 model machinery the new text invokes. The Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront (2003) A&A 412, 567 paper (DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361:20031539) is the canonical source for the P03 model and the longitudinal expansion coefficients. Adding it inline would close the citation loop on the new content. This is the same class of nit the c3 review surfaced on the (now-fixed) generic IAU homepage URL — a fresh instance.
holes_found.recommendation: Add an inline link to the Capitaine et al. 2003 paper next to 'IAU 2006 P_03 model' — e.g., 'IAU 2006 P03 model' or NASA ADS link. Two lines of text, one new tag.

stronger_arguments.description: The new defender-preemption block is genuinely impressive — it doesn't just close the rescue hatch, it converts the rescue into a worse failure. Three independent moves: (a) compute the actual IAU 2006 angular acceleration from the P03 model and show it is twelve orders of magnitude below the dome's number; (b) compute what the dome's number, taken at face value as an acceleration, would produce as a one-year increment to the precession rate (factor 2×10⁷ runaway) and note VLBI/LLR would have detected it; (c) point out the dome's own page text labels this a 'rate' matching the 25,772-yr cycle, so the acceleration rescue is a retreat the published claim doesn't make. Moves (a) and (b) together force the defender into a trilemma: the units are wrong, OR the value is unobservable, OR Earth's precession has been silently runaway-accelerating for decades undetected. Move (c) closes the trilemma's first horn by tying the defender to the dome's own framing. This is what a confirmed-and-then-checkmated rebuttal looks like. No additional structural argument needed.
sources: Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront 2003 A&A 412, 567 (DOI:10.1051/0004-6361:20031539) — the actual P03 source

kernel_of_truth.description: Earth genuinely is a precessing gyroscope; classical mechanics gives the precession via τ = dL/dt applied to a spinning body with luni-solar gravitational torques on the equatorial bulge (data/wins.json:WIN-021 detail_evidence — the closing paragraph correctly notes 'caused by gravitational torques from the Sun and Moon on Earth's equatorial bulge'). The 25,772-year period the dome implicitly references is an authentic, beautiful observable known since Hipparchus and precisely captured by IAU 2006. Identifying precession as a fundamental geophysical observable worthy of prediction is itself a sensible move — many flat-earth defenders never even get to this category of evidence.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Being conceptually in-bounds doesn't save the WIN because the actual claim (data/wins.json:WIN-021 detail_claim = 'Gyroscopic precession rate matches dome model prediction') fails on three independent grounds documented in the same detail_evidence: (1) wrong units — rad/s² is angular acceleration, not the angular velocity a 'rate' requires; (2) wrong number — 37% below the IAU 2006 value of 7.7257e-12 rad/s computed from 50.2881 arcsec/yr; (3) no derivation — raw-text/02-wins.txt:106 presents 4.87e-12 statically with no calculation path. The post-hoc structural critique in data/wins.json:WIN-021 detail_verdict_text adds a fourth grade of failure: even if (1)–(3) were resolved, the value is textbook physics and so cannot have been 'predicted' in any meaningful sense under the dome's own definition of timestamping. The conceptual kernel sits entirely upstream of the failed claim; the claim itself misses on every axis that would matter for a confirmation.

cycle_comparison.changes_since_cycle_3: All three c3 holes resolved. (a) MAJOR HOLE-001 (duplicated sentences): FIXED — the 'Even correcting the units charitably...' sentence and the 'monitor.py does not include...' sentence each now appear exactly once in current detail_evidence; the duplication that c3 caught is gone. (b) MINOR HOLE-002 (IAU URL generic homepage): FIXED — link upgraded to https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf which directly substantiates the 50.2881 arcsec/yr value. (c) MODERATE HOLE-003 (defender escape hatch left open): FIXED, and the fix significantly exceeds the c3 recommendation. c3 asked for '~1 sentence preempting the we meant acceleration defense.' The integrated text instead added an ~600-char block computing the IAU 2006 P03 actual angular acceleration, comparing it to the dome's value (twelve orders of magnitude), showing the runaway consequence of the dome's number taken at face value, and tying the rescue to the dome's own published 'rate' framing. This is a substantively stronger preemption than c3 sketched.
cycle_comparison.new_issues_found: 2 minor (t² coefficient factor-of-2 precision nit; missing inline citation to Capitaine et al. 2003 for the new P03 reference). Both minor, neither breaks the argument.
cycle_comparison.remaining_from_cycle_3: None. All c3 unresolved items closed.

deeper_analysis:
WIN-021 was a textbook example of the change-detection pipeline working as designed. c2 found a moderate stronger-argument angle (preempt the 'we meant acceleration' defense). c3 escalated it after the original c2 recommendation went unintegrated for ~4 weeks and the change-detection scan happened to flag the WIN for an unrelated editorial bug. The decider's response in the c3-to-c4 window converted both findings into integrated prose: the duplicate-sentences bug got cleaned up, the IAU URL got upgraded, AND the previously-stranded c2 stronger_argument finally made it into the rendered text — now expanded with new quantitative work (P03 model angular acceleration, 12-order magnitude comparison, 2×10⁷ runaway analysis) that goes well beyond the original recommendation. The integration trajectory matters: c2 saw the gap, c3 reframed it as a moderate hole, the c4 prose now closes it with a structural argument that traps any defender into a trilemma. This is the kind of cumulative review-driven strengthening the pipeline is designed to produce.

The two minor nits I'm flagging in c4 are housekeeping, not substance. The t² coefficient factor-of-2 elision is the kind of thing a physicist might note when reading the paragraph closely; the math result quoted (10⁻²⁴ rad/s²) is in fact the correct order of magnitude for the actual angular acceleration, so the argument doesn't break — but the parenthetical wording '(≈1 arcsec/century²), which converts to roughly 10⁻²⁴' conflates the polynomial coefficient (5.3×10⁻²⁵) with the second-derivative (1.05×10⁻²⁴). Either way the 'twelve orders of magnitude' framing is conservative. The missing inline citation to Capitaine et al. 2003 is the new-content analog of the c3 minor URL nit on the velocity figure — closing it would be one tag in HTML.

Verdict 'Misleading' continues to hold. The new content arguably makes a case for 'Refuted by Data' (the trilemma is harder than 'misleading' suggests), but I would not advocate the upgrade in this run: the structural post-hoc/transcription critique in detail_verdict_text is the load-bearing argument and that critique is fully captured by 'Misleading.' Promoting to 'Refuted by Data' would require either a verdict-taxonomy holistic review or an explicit decider decision on the rate-vs-acceleration framing; the current verdict is defensible.

citations_verified: IAU 2006 angular velocity computed: 50.2881 arcsec/yr × π/648000 rad/arcsec / (365.25 × 86400 s/yr) = 7.7257×10⁻¹² rad/s. Period 2π/ω = 25,772 yr. Matches text claims.

IAU 2006 P03 t² coefficient: Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront (2003) A&A 412, 567 give psi_A = 5038.481507·t − 1.0790069·t² − 0.00114045·t³ + ... arcsec, t in Julian centuries. |t² coefficient| ≈ 1.079, rounds to 'about 1.' Angular acceleration d²psi/dt² = 2 × |t² coef| ≈ 2.158 arcsec/century² ≈ 1.05×10⁻²⁴ rad/s² (or 5.3×10⁻²⁵ if interpreting the parenthetical's '≈1 arcsec/century²' as the bare coefficient). Both readings cluster at 'roughly 10⁻²⁴'.
Twelve orders of magnitude: log10(4.87×10⁻¹² / 1.05×10⁻²⁴) = 12.67; log10(4.87×10⁻¹² / 5.25×10⁻²⁵) = 12.97. Either rounds to 'twelve.'
Runaway factor: 4.87×10⁻¹² × (365.25 × 86400) = 1.537×10⁻⁴ rad/s increment over a year; 1.537×10⁻⁴ / 7.73×10⁻¹² = 1.99×10⁷. Text claims '~2×10⁷.' Verified.

IAU 2006 Resolution B1 PDF URL: https://www.iau.org/static/resolutions/IAU2006_Resol1.pdf — verified live (used for the 50.2881 arcsec/yr citation), upgrade from the c3 generic homepage is correctly applied.
citations_unchecked: Capitaine, Wallace, Chapront (2003) A&A 412, 567 — the P03 model canonical source. Not directly linked in current text; would address HOLE-002 if added. DOI: 10.1051/0004-6361:20031539.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender who reads the c4 text closely could mount: 'Your trilemma — wrong units, OR unobservable acceleration, OR runaway-undetected-by-VLBI — assumes my predicted angular acceleration is meant to be a CONSTANT secular quantity over decades. But under the dome aetheric framework, the 4.87×10⁻¹² rad/s² figure can be read as an instantaneous-in-time effective acceleration that integrates to zero over the precession cycle (a kind of harmonic perturbation around the mean rate), not a constant secular term. Your VLBI runaway argument then fails because the integral over decades is zero, and your 12-orders-of-magnitude argument fails because you're comparing the wrong physical quantity (you're comparing a peak-to-mean perturbation amplitude against a secular acceleration). The IAU 2006 P03 model captures only the secular trend, not the instantaneous fluctuation amplitude — you've left that channel unexamined.' This is the strongest remaining defender move I can construct against the c4 text. It is weak in two ways: (1) the dome's published WIN entry says 'rate,' not 'time-varying perturbation amplitude,' so this is a clear retreat from the published claim — which the c4 text already anticipates by tying defenders to 'rate' framing; (2) no dome documentation describes a harmonic-perturbation model anywhere, so the defender is constructing an interpretation post-hoc to rescue the value. But it is the strongest remaining angle: it doesn't require defending the units literally, doesn't require defending a constant secular acceleration that VLBI would catch, and asks the reviewer to disprove the existence of an unexamined channel — the classic argumentum ad ignorantiam move.

cross_win_consistency.notes: Cross-checked against the four other WINs that mention 'precess' in their detail_evidence: WIN-006 (NP pre-1990 linear drift — no precession reference in detail_evidence prose, only in the broader pole-motion context), WIN-020 (lunar 18.6-yr cycle — gears framework, no precession in expanded text), WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis dimness — uses 25,772-year precession cycle to argue south celestial pole motion toward Canopus by ~14,000 AD; compatible with our WIN-021 25,772-yr cycle reference), WIN-051 (tidal N2 period — passing mention of 'apsidal precession' as one of the orbital mechanics dynamics the dome cannot explain; compatible with our standard-physics framing). The new c4 invocation of the IAU 2006 P03 model is new to the corpus — no other WIN cites Capitaine et al. yet, so no cross-WIN citation-consistency concern. Aggregate code_analysis cluster (monitoring:none, post_hoc:true, derives_from_dome:false) covers ~38 WINs; WIN-021 remains the only one in that cluster making a numerical rad/s-class claim. The trivial-transcription argument we invoke in WIN-021's detail_verdict_text is the same Part 3.1b argument applied consistently across the cluster (e.g., WIN-001 Tesla 11.78 Hz, WIN-050/051 tidal periods, WIN-033 25,772-yr precession cycle); no internal contradiction.

claims_checked: IAU 2006 P03 t² coefficient ≈1 arcsec/century² (verified at 1.079)
Conversion to ≈10⁻²⁴ rad/s² (verified at 5.3×10⁻²⁵ as bare coef, 1.05×10⁻²⁴ as actual angular acceleration; either rounds to 10⁻²⁴ neighborhood)
'Twelve orders of magnitude smaller' (verified at 12.67–12.97 depending on interpretation)
